units.title: Explicit login exchange overview
units.reason: Section 6 introduces explicit login as an ELS-based procedure using FLOGI or PLOGI in a new Exchange, with LS_ACC returning responder Service Parameters.

evidence_refs.quote: Explicit Login is accomplished using a Login (FLOGI or PLOGI) ELS
The LS_ACC contains the Service Parameters of the Responder

units.title: Explicit Fabric Login request
units.reason: Explicit Fabric Login requires an Nx_Port to send FLOGI in a new Exchange; the payload carries Service Parameters, N_Port_Name, and Node_Name, and the D_ID is the well-known F_Port address.

evidence_refs.quote: The Nx_Port shall transmit the FLOGI in a new Exchange.
The Nx_Port shall assign an OX_ID and
set the D_ID to the well-known F_Port address (i.e., FFFFFEh).

units.title: Explicit Fabric Login response branches
units.reason: The Fabric Login procedure has distinct implementation branches for Fabric LS_ACC, point-to-point LS_ACC from another Nx_Port, F_BSY, P_BSY, F_RJT, P_RJT, LS_RJT, no response, and self-connect discard.

evidence_refs.quote: Responses to Explicit Fabric Login
LS_ACC reply Sequence
LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI, and the Common Ser
F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
P_RJT Sequence, with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
No Response may indicate a delivery error
If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name

units.title: Fabric relogin change handling
units.reason: When Fabric relogin reveals changed N_Port_ID, F_Port_Name, or Fabric_Name, the Nx_Port must implicitly logout existing Nx_Port relationships, wait R_A_TOV, and require new N_Port Logins before resuming communication.

evidence_refs.quote: Relogin with the Fabric
Nx_Port detects that the N_Port_ID, F_Port_Name, and/or the Fabric_Name has changed
the Nx_Port shall implicitly logout with all Nx_Ports and wait an R_A_TOV timeout
After waiting the timeout period, new N_Port Logins are
required before the Nx_Port may communicate with other Nx_Ports.

units.title: Explicit N_Port Login with Fabric present
units.reason: Section 6.3 states N_Port Login follows Fabric Login, and section 6.3.2 defines explicit N_Port Login via PLOGI Link Service Sequence sent within an Exchange to a destination Nx_Port.

evidence_refs.quote: N_Port Login follows the Fabric Login procedure.
The destination Nx_Port explicit Login procedure requires transmission of a N_Port Login (PLOGI)
Link Service Sequence.  The PLOGI is sent within an Exchange with an assigned OX_ID

units.title: N_Port Login response branches with Fabric present
units.reason: PLOGI with a Fabric present has implementation branches for normal LS_ACC, F_BSY, F_RJT, P_BSY, P_RJT, inbound PLOGI collision, LS_RJT, no response, and self-connection case.

evidence_refs.quote: Responses to N_Port Login - Fabric present
LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI
F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
P_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
PLOGI Sequence.  The D_ID is the N_Port_ID of receiving Nx_Port.
LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
No Response may indicate a delivery error
If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name

units.title: Point-to-point N_Port Login initiation without Fabric
units.reason: Without a Fabric, only one Nx_Port needs to originate PLOGI, normally the one with the highest N_Port_Name if names were learned during Fabric Login.

evidence_refs.quote: N_Port Login - No Fabric present
Only one Nx_Port in the point-to-point connection is required to transmit a PLOGI.
N_Port_Names are exchanged during Fabric Login, the Nx_Port with the highest N_Port_Name shall

units.title: N_Port Login response branches without Fabric
units.reason: In point-to-point mode without a Fabric, PLOGI handling includes LS_ACC, P_BSY, P_RJT, LS_RJT, inbound PLOGI collision, no response, and self-connection case.

evidence_refs.quote: Responses to N_Port Login - No Fabric present
LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI
P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
P_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
PLOGI Sequence.  The D_ID is the address identifier of receiving Nx_Port.
No Response may indicate a delivery error
If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name

units.title: Process Login after N_Port Login
units.reason: PRLI establishes the operating environment between related process groups, runs after successful N_Port Login and before other FC-4 transfers, and may establish or modify image pairs with PRLI plus LS_ACC.

evidence_refs.quote: The Process Login (PRLI) ELS request shall be used to establish the operating environment
PRLI, if required, is performed after N_Port Login is successful and prior to other FC-4 transfers.
An image pair may be established or modified with a PRLI request and LS_ACC reply Sequence set.

units.title: PRLI response and retry handling
units.reason: PRLI establishment is not considered complete until local action plus LS_ACC; if the requester is not logged in, the responder returns LOGO or LS_RJT with N_Port Login required; several reject/busy outcomes allow retry, and no valid response should trigger retry.

evidence_refs.quote: The Nx_Port originating the PRLI request shall not consider the image pair to be established until it
quest does not consider the image pair to be established until the necessary action is taken at the
it shall reply with a LOGO ELS Sequence, or with an LS_RJT ELS Sequence
"Unable to perform command request" and a reason code explanation of "N_Port Login required".
If an LS_RJT, P_BSY, F_BSY, P_RJT, or F_RJT response is re
ceived to a PRLI request, the PRLI request may be retried until the image pair is established.
shall not assume that the requested action has or has not taken place. If the Nx_Port that originates
a PRLI request receives no valid response, the Nx_Port should retry the request.

units.title: Common Service Parameter applicability table for login procedures
units.reason: Table 150 is directly referenced by FLOGI and PLOGI text and shows which Common Service Parameters matter in PLOGI, FLOGI, and FLOGI LS_ACC contexts; for example Buffer-to-buffer Credit and N_Port/F_Port are applicable, while the Virtual Fabrics bit is not applicable to PLOGI but is applicable to FLOGI and FLOGI LS_ACC.

units.title: Class Service Parameter applicability table for login procedures
units.reason: Table 155 is the class-parameter counterpart referenced by login sections and shows which Class Service Parameters are meaningful in PLOGI, FLOGI, and FLOGI LS_ACC; for example Class Validity applies everywhere, Initial Responder Process_Associator applies to PLOGI but not FLOGI, and CR_TOV appears only in FLOGI LS_ACC Class 1 applicability.
